column1: Which germ layer forms the epithelial lining of the primitive gut tube?
What structure connects the midgut region to the yolk sac during early development?
At approximately what embryonic day does the respiratory diverticulum first form?
The respiratory diverticulum is a ventral evagination of which specific gut tube subdivision?
Which germ layer gives rise to the epithelium of the respiratory system?
From which tissue are the bronchial cartilages and smooth muscles derived?
During which week of development does the oropharyngeal membrane typically rupture?
The cloacal membrane typically ruptures during the seventh week to form the orifices of which two systems?
Which germ layer is the source of the extramural digestive gland epithelium, such as the liver and pancreas?
Secondary bronchial buds represent the rudiments of which future anatomical structures?
Tertiary bronchial buds represent the rudiments of which future anatomical structures?
By which year of life do alveoli typically stop forming?
Which gut tube subdivision gives rise to the stomach and the proximal half of the duodenum?
Which gut tube subdivision forms the jejunum, ileum, and the ascending colon?
Which gut tube subdivision forms the descending colon and the sigmoid colon?
The proximal end, or stem, of the respiratory diverticulum differentiates into which two structures?
What is the name of the passageway that opens into the pharynx at the original point of the respiratory diverticulum's evagination?
Which specific mesoderm layer differentiates into the lamina propria and muscular walls of the gastrointestinal tract?
The epithelium of the oral cavity and the lower part of the anal canal is derived from which germ layer?
The lining epithelium of the auditory (Eustachian) tubes is a derivative of which germ layer?
The parenchyma of the tonsils is derived from which embryonic germ layer?
In the respiratory system, the structural basis for gas exchange is found within the _____.
Kartagener syndrome is caused by mutations in which specific gene?
What is the primary cellular defect in the respiratory tract of individuals with Kartagener syndrome?
In patients with Primary Ciliary Dyskinesia (PCD), what percentage of affected individuals exhibit situs inversus?
Which embryonic process yields a three-dimensional embryo with a tube-within-a-tube body plan?
At what stage of lung development do terminal bronchioles typically form?
The first terminal sacs (primitive alveoli) begin to form at approximately which week of development?
Which embryonic membrane caps the cranial end of the foregut?
Which embryonic membrane caps the caudal end of the hindgut?
The first bifurcation of the respiratory diverticulum, occurring on days 26 to 28, creates the _____.
Which structure separates the primitive pericardial cavity from the pleural cavities during development?
The midgut forms approximately how much of the transverse colon?
The hindgut forms approximately how much of the transverse colon?
The upper two thirds of the anorectal canal is derived from which gut subdivision?
Which germ layer gives rise to the epithelial components of the thyroid and parathyroid glands?
The respiratory diverticulum grows into which specific type of mesenchyme?
The abdominal esophagus, stomach, and superior duodenum are suspended by both _____ and ventral mesenteries.
By the end of which week is the gut tube fully formed and the yolk sac neck reduced to the vitelline duct?
Which specific embryonic structure gives rise to the liver cords and gallbladder?
The abdominal foregut includes which part of the duodenum?
Which specific structures close off the pericardioperitoneal canals to separate the thoracic and abdominal cavities?
During gastrulation, which germ layer is partitioned into broad regions along the cranial-caudal axis?
The lining epithelium of the tympanic cavity is derived from which germ layer?
The lining epithelium of the urinary bladder is primarily derived from which germ layer?
The respiratory system epithelium is of _____ origin.
Which gut subdivision contains the pharynx, located cranial to the respiratory diverticulum?
Which transcription factors and morphogens released by cardiogenic mesoderm stimulate Shh in the ventral foregut?
Which gut subdivision gives rise to the jejunum and ileum?
Terminal sacs begin to differentiate into mature alveoli starting at which week of development?
The lower part of the anal canal is derived from which germ layer?
Which embryonic structure represents the future epidermis that covers the outer surface of the embryo?
The neck of the yolk sac is constricted into the vitelline duct as the _____ forms.
The connective tissue elements of the respiratory system are derived from _____ mesoderm.
Which part of the duodenum is derived from the midgut?
